name: Tito Y Tita School Bus
districts_or_customers: Likely private K-12 schools / parent contracts in Miami-Dade (Hialeah/Miami area); not a Miami-Dade County Public Schools contractor at this scale
recent_news: No recent M&A or news found; appears to be a single-van micro-operator registered ~2018. Searches surface a similarly named 'Tita School Bus Inc.' in Hialeah (W. Gomez area) advertising 35+ years experience, which may be a related/affiliated family business or namesake confusion.
fit_notes: Too small for a Summit tuck-in on its own (single van, ~$89K revenue, 2 employees). Only relevant if rolled into a Miami-Dade micro-operator consolidation play; no standalone strategic value.

name: Tony's School Bus
districts_or_customers: Likely private school / parent-contract routes in Hialeah / Miami-Dade County
recent_news: No M&A, news, or website found. Address (1052 W 79th St, Hialeah) and phone (305-753-1448) appear only in generic Yellow Pages / Manta-style directory listings for 'School Bus Service.'
fit_notes: Single-bus, 2-employee Hialeah micro-operator. Not a standalone Summit tuck-in target; only of interest as part of a broader Miami-Dade private-route roll-up.

name: Yanet School Bus

fit_notes: Single-bus, 2-employee operator. Too small to be a standalone Summit tuck-in; only relevant as part of a Miami-Dade micro-operator consolidation.

name: Yordy School Bus
principals: Jorge Yordanka (Manager); registered as 'Yordy School Bus Inc.' with FL Sunbiz
districts_or_customers: Private school / parent-contract routes in Cutler Bay / South Miami-Dade County (Homestead-to-Cutler-Bay corridor noted in directory listings)
recent_news: FL Sunbiz registration P08000072866 confirms 'Yordy School Bus Inc.' (filed 2008). Yellow Pages listing references 34 years in business and a second address at 19301 SW 108th Ave, Cutler Bay, but flagged 'closed now' in directory—operating status uncertain. No M&A or news.
fit_notes: Single-bus, 2-employee South Miami-Dade operator with possible inactive status. Not a standalone Summit tuck-in target; only of interest if part of a broader Miami-Dade private-route consolidation.
